nodes.title: Depth with support
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §1.1.}
  For a ring \(A\) and a closed subset \(Z \subset \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), write \(\operatorname{depth}_Z(A) \ge d\) if there exists an \(A\)-regular sequence \(a_1, \ldots, a_d \in A\) vanishing on \(Z\).

nodes.title: Purity for flat cohomology
Étale cohomological purity
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §1.1 (Theorem on absolute cohomological purity).}
  For a regular local ring \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) and a commutative finite étale \(R\)-group scheme \(G\) whose order is invertible in \(R\),
  \[
    H^i_{\mathfrak{m}}(R, G) = 0 \quad \text{for } i < 2\dim(R).
  \]
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{}
  This is proved by Gabber (first in the framework of Thomason's \(K\)-theoretic approach, then in \cite{ILO14}). The paper gives a new proof reducing to positive characteristic via perfectoid techniques.

nodes.title: Étale semipurity
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §1.1.}
  For a Noetherian local ring \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) and a commutative finite étale \(R\)-group scheme \(G\),
  \[
    H^i_{\mathfrak{m}}(R, G) = 0 \quad \text{for } i < \vdim(R).
  \]
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{thm:abs_coh_pur}
  Deduced from \cref{thm:abs_coh_pur} using a Lefschetz hyperplane theorem in local étale cohomology and induction on the number of generators of the defining ideal of \(\widehat{R}\).

nodes.title: Perfectoid purity
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §1.1.}
  For a perfectoid \(\mathbb{Z}_p\)-algebra \(A\), a commutative finite locally free \(A\)-group scheme \(G\) of \(p\)-power order, and a closed subset \(Z \subset \operatorname{Spec}(A/pA)\) with \(\operatorname{depth}_Z(A) \ge d\),
  \[
    H^i_Z(A, G) = 0 \quad \text{for } i < d.
  \]
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{thm:arc_descent}
  The flat cohomology \(R\Gamma_Z(A, G)\) is computed via the key formula
  \[
    R\Gamma_Z(A, G) \cong R\Gamma_Z(\mathbb{A}_{\mathrm{Inf}}(A), \mathbb{M}(G))^{V-1},
  \]
  where \(\mathbb{M}(G)\) is the prismatic Dieudonné module of \(G\). Since \(p\) is a nonzerodivisor in \(\mathbb{A}_{\mathrm{Inf}}(A)\), the sequence \(\xi, a_1, \ldots, a_d\) (where \(\xi\) generates \(\ker(\theta)\)) is regular in \(\mathbb{A}_{\mathrm{Inf}}(A)\) and vanishes on \(Z\). Expressing \(\mathbb{M}(G)\) as the cokernel of a map between finite projective \(\mathbb{A}_{\mathrm{Inf}}(A)\)-modules, the vanishing of quasi-coherent cohomology with supports in the required range gives the result. The key formula is proved using \cref{thm:arc_descent}.

nodes.title: Gabber's conjecture: torsion Picard groups
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §1.1 (Gabber's conjectures, part (a)).}
  For a Noetherian local ring \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) that is a complete intersection with \(\dim(R) \ge 3\), the torsion subgroup of the Picard group of the punctured spectrum \(U_R = \operatorname{Spec}(R) \setminus \{\mathfrak{m}\}\) vanishes:
  \[
    \Pic(U_R)_{\mathrm{tors}} = 0.
  \]
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{}
  This follows from \cref{thm:main} applied to group schemes of the form \(\mu_n\) and \(\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z}\) via the exact sequence relating Picard groups and flat cohomology.

nodes.title: Gabber's conjecture: Brauer group purity
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §1.1 (Gabber's conjectures, part (b)).}
  For a Noetherian local ring \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) that is a complete intersection with \(\dim(R) \ge 4\), or with \(R\) regular and \(\dim(R) \ge 2\),
  \[
    \Br(R) \xrightarrow{\sim} \Br(U_R),
  \]
  where \(U_R = \operatorname{Spec}(R) \setminus \{\mathfrak{m}\}\).
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{}
  Follows from \cref{thm:main} applied to group schemes related to \(\mathbb{G}_m\)-torsors and the Kummer sequence.

nodes.title: Global Brauer purity
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §1.1 (global Brauer purity theorem).}
  For a Noetherian scheme \(X\) and a closed subset \(Z \subset X\) such that each local ring \(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}\) with \(z \in Z\) is either a complete intersection of dimension \(\ge 4\) or regular of dimension \(\ge 2\),
  \[
    H^2(X, \mathbb{G}_m)_{\mathrm{tors}} \xrightarrow{\sim} H^2(X \setminus Z, \mathbb{G}_m)_{\mathrm{tors}}.
  \]
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{}
  Globalizes \cref{cor:gabber_brauer} using a Mayer--Vietoris argument in étale/fppf cohomology.

nodes.title: Excision for fppf cohomology
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §1.2.}
  For a ring \(R\), a map \(f \colon A \to A'\) of animated \(R\)-algebras, and a finitely generated ideal \(I = (a_1, \ldots, a_r) \subset \pi_0(A)\) such that \(f\) induces an isomorphism on iterated derived \(I\)-adic completions, and every commutative finite locally free \(R\)-group scheme \(G\),
  \[
    R\Gamma_I(A, G) \xrightarrow{\sim} R\Gamma_I(A', G).
  \]
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{}
  Deduced from the positive characteristic case of the key formula via animated deformation theory.

nodes.title: fppf equals fpqc for finite locally free coefficients
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §1.2.}
  For a ring \(R\) and a commutative finite locally free \(R\)-group scheme \(G\), the functor \(A \mapsto R\Gamma_{\mathrm{fppf}}(A, G)\) satisfies hyperdescent in the fpqc topology on animated \(R\)-algebras.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{}
  Proved via animated deformation theory and the positive characteristic case of the key formula.

nodes.title: \(p\)-complete arc descent
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §1.2.}
  For a \(p\)-complete arc hypercover \(A \to A^{\bullet}\) of perfectoid \(\mathbb{Z}_p\)-algebras, a closed \(Z \subset \operatorname{Spec}(A/pA)\), and a commutative finite locally free \(A\)-group scheme \(G\) of \(p\)-power order,
  \[
    R\Gamma_Z(A, G) \xrightarrow{\sim} R\lim_{\Delta} R\Gamma_Z(A^{\bullet}, G),
  \]
  where \(\Delta\) is the simplex category.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{}
  This is the \(p\)-complete arc hyperdescent for the flat cohomology side of the key formula. An important input is the analogous \(p\)-complete arc descent for the structure sheaf functor on perfectoid rings.

nodes.title: Iterated adic continuity
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §1.2.}
  For a ring \(R\), an animated \(R\)-algebra \(A\), elements \(a_1, \ldots, a_r \in A\) such that \(A\) agrees with its iterated derived \(a_i\)-adic completion for \(i = 1, \ldots, r\), and a commutative finite locally free \(R\)-group scheme \(G\),
  \[
    R\Gamma(A, G) \xrightarrow{\sim} R\lim_{n > 0} \left( R\Gamma(A/^{\mathbf{L}} (a_1^n, \ldots, a_r^n), G) \right).
  \]
  The \(p\)-adic continuity of \cref{thm:p_adic_continuity} is the special case \(r = 1\), \(a_1 = p\).
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{thm:p_adic_continuity}
  Deduced from the positive characteristic case of the key formula \eqref{eqn:key-formula} via crystalline Dieudonné theory: after identifying \(R\Gamma((-)[1/p], G)\) with étale cohomology and applying arc descent, one works along \(\{p = 0\}\) to reduce to \(p\)-Henselian and then \(\mathbb{F}_p\)-algebras using the general \(p\)-adic continuity result of \cref{thm:p_adic_continuity}.

nodes.title: \(p\)-adic continuity
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §1.2.}
  For a prime \(p\), a ring \(R\), a commutative finite locally free \(R\)-group scheme \(G\) of \(p\)-power order, and an animated \(R\)-algebra \(A\) for which \(\pi_0(A)\) is \(p\)-Henselian,
  \[
    R\Gamma_{\mathrm{fppf}}(A, G) \xrightarrow{\sim} R\lim_{n > 0} R\Gamma_{\mathrm{fppf}}(A/^{\mathbf{L}} p^n, G).
  \]
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{}
  Established by a direct attack combining \(\ell\)-adic methods (identifying \(R\Gamma(A[1/p], G)\) with étale cohomology) and animated deformation theory to reduce to \(\mathbb{F}_p\)-algebras.

nodes.title: Complete intersection local ring
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, \S1.1 Notation and conventions.}
  A Noetherian local ring \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is a \emph{complete intersection} if its \(\mathfrak{m}\)-adic completion \(\widehat{R}\) is a quotient of a regular local ring \(\widetilde{R}\) by a regular sequence. Here regular sequences are in the sense of EGA (no condition on the quotients being nonzero). Equivalently, the ideal cutting \(\widehat{R}\) out of \(\widetilde{R}\) is generated by a regular sequence.

nodes.title: Commutative finite flat group scheme
nodes.statement: A \emph{commutative finite flat \(R\)-group scheme} \(G\) is a finite flat \(R\)-scheme equipped with a commutative group structure over \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\). Equivalently, \(G\) corresponds to a finite locally free commutative \(R\)-Hopf algebra.

nodes.title: Flat cohomology with support
nodes.statement: For a ring \(R\), a closed subset \(Z \subset \operatorname{Spec}(R)\), and a commutative finite flat \(R\)-group scheme \(G\), the \emph{flat cohomology with support} \(H^i_Z(R, G)\) is the \(i\)-th cohomology group of the derived functor of sections supported on \(Z\) computed in the fppf topology. When \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) is a local ring and \(Z = \{\mathfrak{m}\}\), we write \(H^i_{\mathfrak{m}}(R, G)\).

nodes.title: Perfectoid ring
Virtual dimension
nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: Cesnavicius--Scholze, §3.1.}
  Let \((R, \mathfrak{m})\) be a Noetherian local ring. By Cohen's theorem, the \(\mathfrak{m}\)-adic completion \(\widehat{R} \cong \widetilde{R}/I\) for a complete regular local ring \(\widetilde{R}\) and an ideal \(I \subset \widetilde{R}\). The \emph{virtual dimension} of \(R\) is
  \[
    \vdim(R) \coloneqq \dim(\widetilde{R}) - \mu(I),
  \]
  where \(\mu(I)\) is the minimal number of generators of \(I\). This is independent of the Cohen presentation. One has \(\vdim(R) \le \dim(R)\) with equality if and only if \(R\) is a complete intersection.
